title: method_ok("error", ...) is a misleading name for error returns
description: message_query (line 2025) and message_query_changes (line 2080) use 'return method_ok("error", json\!({...}))' to return error responses. method_ok is named for success. A maintainer reading this is confused. The existing method_error() helper doesn't support custom bodies, so the fix is either: use ("error".to_string(), json\!({...})) directly, or add a method_error_detail(type, body) helper. Suggested rewrite: ("error".to_string(), serde_json::json\!({"type": "unsupportedFilter", "description": "..."}))
close_reason: Fixed: replaced method_ok("error", ...) with ("error".to_string(), json!({...})) in both message_query and message_query_changes
